Private — Career Guidance Test
Maeve
Senior Manager, Strategy  ·  Biotech  ·  Mid-career (5–15 years)
She has spent over a decade building toward this moment in her career, and a real path to a much bigger role has just opened up. But something else significant is unfolding in her life at the same time, on a timeline she cannot control. She is trying to make a major professional decision without knowing how the other part of her life will unfold, and she has not found many women who have navigated something like this.
Why we thought of you She asked to hear from women further along in their career who have made a big decision while something significant and uncertain was happening in their life at the same time. We chose you because your experience is directly relevant to what she is weighing, even if the specifics differ.
Please take a moment to read her question carefully. The more context you have, the more useful your response will be to her.
Read her full question ⌄
Her situation
I'm a Senior Manager in Strategy at a biotech company, in a high-performance environment where timing and optics matter. My company has just opened a real path to a much bigger role, one I've been working toward for a while. At the same time, I'm in the middle of a fertility treatment process, on a timeline I can't control. My employer doesn't know.
The challenge
If the treatment works, I'd be stepping into a much bigger role while pregnant or about to go on leave. If it doesn't, I may have let something important pass me by. I don't know how to make a career decision that depends on an outcome I can't control yet.
What she is looking for
I want to hear from women who have made a big professional decision while something this uncertain was happening in their life at the same time. How did they weigh the options? How did they handle the uncertainty? What helped them decide? Could concerns like mine be unfounded fear? What did they end up choosing, and looking back, are they glad?
Something else worth knowing
My pregnancy could be complicated given my medical history, and this is likely my last realistic chance to try.
What would be most useful to her
Their personal experience A way of thinking about it Questions to sit with
Please open and read her full question before continuing.
About this experiment ⌄
This is a private experiment. Lucy is running this personally and will be the only other person to see what you share before it reaches Maeve. Depending on how much you want to say, this whole experience should take no more than a few minutes. We have tried to make it as easy as possible.
Technical issue? Email lucydesigning@gmail.com

Step 3 of 5
Share what you know.
Voice is encouraged. It tends to feel more personal and is easier to give honestly. Written is equally welcome. Aim for around 2 to 4 minutes if speaking, or 200 to 300 words if writing.

Thank you. Maeve will receive your message shortly.

What you shared will reach someone who is sitting with a hard question. She will hear from a few different women, each from their own angle, and yours may spark something she has not been able to put words to yet.

We are grateful you said yes. When you find yourself in a place of needing help, we hope you come back.
